entries.definition: A named bounded predicate requiring historical live evidence, current no-live status, and typed transition provenance.
A typed record connecting the prior licensed state, the exhaustion transition, and the resulting current state.
A DLE transition certified not to delete, rewrite, retype, or change the identity of a specified residual register.
One of TARGET_PRESENCE, HISTORICAL, MEMORY, SIGNAL, CAUSAL, INFORMATION, or GENERIC_TRACE.
An explicit typed map between residual classes with declared domain, codomain, and preservation obligations.
A relation permitted only when type identity or an admissible translation contract is supplied.
Two models with validated schemas, a non-label-only distinctness basis, and a declared shared fragment.
A certificate that two admissible models have equal interpretations on every symbol in a declared shared fragment.
A bounded model-pair witness that a residual fact is not determined by the declared shared fragment.
A sufficient single-residual witness for raw-relative obstruction, without a converse or completeness claim.
A typed archive/history token that records prior status but is not itself a current guard token.
A typed event that creates a new current token from an explicitly recorded activation source.
A token currently licensed to serve as a live guard in the active registry state.
